data.narrative:
Strider Technologies is a Series C-stage defense and security company that has raised $117M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 12 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage peers, including 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms. Notable comparables include Quantum-Systems ($8.0B, Series C), Helsing ($18.0B, Series D), and Saronic ($9.2B, Series D), with the peer set ranging from $300M to $18.0B.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using $117M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement, implies a valuation of $199M. Peer-median analysis across the 12-company Defense & Security cohort yields a median valuation of $1.8B, reflecting the sector's capital intensity and strategic value. The peer range ($300M to $18.0B) is wide, reflecting stage and maturity dispersion within the comp set.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $169M (low) to $2.0B (high), with a median of $987M. The substantial gap between stage-progression and peer-median results reflects uncertainty around Strider's relative positioning within its peer cohort. Key sensitivities include technology maturity, customer concentration, and path to Series D funding. Further revenue and profitability data would refine the upper and lower bounds.
